content:
Introduction
M57TU1-TOP Engine
Diesel engine with two-stage supercharging
The most powerful diesel engine used in the
E60 will be the M57D30TU1-TOP (model
designation 535d).
The engine is characterized by outstanding
power output characteristics. The power
output is 200 kW/272 bhp. The maximum
torque of 560 Nm is developed at 2000 rpm.
A torque of 500 Nm is available at an engine
speed of 1500 rpm.
Changes
Compared to the M57TU1-EURO 4 engine,
the following changes and new developments
have been implemented on the following
components:
• Exhaust turbocharger
• Exhaust system
• Intake air control
• Pistons and connecting rods
• Camshafts
• Belt drive
• Reduced compression ratio
• Increased fuel injector cross section
• Digital diesel electronics DDE 606
1 - Comparison between M57TU1-EURO 4 and M57TU1-TOP

The engine number is located on the engine
block above the engine oil cooler.

System components
M57TU1-TOP Engine
Modified components
Exhaust turbocharger
Two-stage turbocharging solves the conflict
of interest between response at low engine
speeds and the availability of high power
reserves during motorway driving.
Two series-connected turbochargers are
used. A small turbocharger for the high
pressure stage and a larger turbocharger for
the low pressure stage. Both turbochargers
have a rigid turbine geometry without
adjustable turbine blades.
The smaller exhaust turbocharger (high
pressure stage) develops high torque and
good response characteristics (t) at low engine
speeds. The larger exhaust turbocharger (low
pressure stage) is responsible for high engine
power output (P).
The max. boost pressure of the overall system
is 2.85 bar (M57TU = max. 2.5 bar). The
turbine speeds are max. 230,000 rpm for the
high pressure stage and max. 170,000 rpm in
the low pressure stage.
A flexible combination of both turbochargers is
possible. Components both on the exhaust
and air intake sides control the variable
interaction of both exhaust turbochargers:
• Turbine control valve (exhaust side)
• Compressor bypass valve (air side)
• Wastegate (exhaust side)
1 - Advantages of two-stage turbocharging
Index
Description
P
Engine output
t
Response characteristic

Exhaust system
The exhaust system has been adapted to the
higher volumetric throughput of the M57TU1-
TOP engine compared to the M57TU1
engine.
For this purpose, the exhaust system is
designed as a dual-flow system between the
diesel particle filter and the rear silencer.
Intake air control
The near-engine arrangement of the intake
silencer is no longer possible due to the
increased demand for space for the exhaust
turbochargers and due to the higher air intake
requirement of the M57TU1-TOP engine.
The intake silencer together with the air
cleaner has therefore been relocated from the
engine to the right-hand wheel arch.
3 - Intake air control
Index
Description
Index Description
Intake snorkel
Line to engine breather
Hot-film air mass meter
Filtered air gaiter

Pistons and connecting rods
The connecting rods are trapezoidal in the
area of the upper connecting rod eye. Due to
the trapezoidal shape, it has been possible to
make the bearing half on the rod side of the
upper connecting rod eye wider, providing
more favourable force input conditions. The
cracked connecting rod is made from forged
steel. The pistons have been adapted to the
trapezoidal shape of the connecting rod. The
geometry of the piston crown bowl has been
slightly modified to adapt to the modified
power output and exhaust requirements.
Camshafts
Built-in camshafts are used. The non-
separable drive gear is connected to the
camshaft. Compared to the previous
camshafts made of clear chill casting, the
built-in camshafts offer a considerable weight
advantage.
Belt drive
A 6 ribbed V-belt assembly with
a mechanical tensioning pulley is used for the
main drive. The A/C compressor is driven by
an Elast-belt. The Elast-belt (4-ribbed belt) is
self-tensioning. It is fitted with a special tool as
part of servicing operations. No other
tensioning facility is provided on the engine.

Turbine control valve
The turbine control valve (4) routes the
exhaust gas through the bypass duct past the
high pressure stage (2) to the low pressure
stage (3). It is operated pneumatically by a
diaphragm unit and can be variably adjusted.
An electropneumatic pressure converter (high
speed EPDW) applies vacuum to the
diaphragm unit.
Compressor bypass valve
The compressor bypass valve (12) controls
the bypass of the high pressure stage (2) on
the air intake side. It is operated pneumatically
by a diaphragm unit. The compressor bypass
valve is either fully opened or completely
closed.
An electric changeover valve (EUV) applies
vacuum to the diaphragm unit.
Wastegate
On reaching the nominal engine output, the
wastegate (5) opens to avoid high boost and
turbine pressures.
A part of the exhaust gas flows via the tailgate
past the turbine of the low pressure stage (3).
It is operated pneumatically by a diaphragm
unit. The wastegate can be variable adjusted.
An electropneumatic pressure converter
(EPDW) applies vacuum to the diaphragm unit.
Index

Switching points
The DDE controls the supercharging
dependent on the engine load and speed. The
switching points stored in a characteristic map
take place in a smooth transition (hysteresis).
The following illustrations show the functions
of the two-stage turbocharging system at full
load:
Lower engine speed range
(up to 1500 rpm)
The turbine wheels of the high pressure and
low pressure stages (6+7) are driven by
exhaust gas. The engine is supercharged
primarily by the high pressure stage (7).
2 - Supercharging function at engine speeds up to 1500 rpm
Index
Description
M57TU1-TOP engine
Turbine control valve with
electropneumatic pressure
converter (EPDW)
Wastegate with electropneumatic
pressure converter (EPDW)
Exhaust gas, to exhaust system
Fresh air, from air cleaner
Exhaust turbocharger (low pressure
stage)
Exhaust turbocharger (high
pressure stage)
Compressor bypass valve with
electric changeover valve (EUV)
Intercooler

Medium engine speed range
(from 1500 to 3250 rpm)
The turbine control valve (2) opens
continuously as the engine speed increases.
Consequently, the flow of exhaust gas
increasingly bypasses the turbine wheel of the
high pressure stage (7). As the engine speed
increases, the engine is supercharged more
and more by the low pressure stage (6).
Upper engine speed range
(from 3250 to 4200 rpm)
The turbine control valve (2) is completely
open. The flow of exhaust gas largely
bypasses the turbine wheel of the high
pressure stage (7). The compressor bypass
valve (8) is open. The engine is supercharged
only by the low pressure stage (6).
3 - Supercharging function at engine speeds from 1500 to 3250 rpm
Index
Description

electropneumatic pressure

stage)

pressure stage)
Compressor bypass valve with
electric changeover valve (EUV)
Intercooler
4 - Supercharging function at engine speeds from 3250 to 4200 rpm

Nominal output range
(as from 4200 rpm)
The engine is supercharged by the low
pressure stage (6). The wastegate (3) opens
as the engine speed increases. A part of the
exhaust gas therefore bypasses the turbine
wheel of the low pressure stage, thus limiting
the turbine speed.
5 - Supercharging function at engine speeds as from 4200 rpm

Vacuum system
The vacuum is routed from the vacuum pump
(1) via a distributor to the near-engine vacuum
accumulator (3). From the vacuum
accumulator, the vacuum is applied to both
electropneumatic pressure converters
(EPDW) (5+6) and to the electric changeover
valve (EUV) (4).
6 - Overview of vacuum system M57TU-TOP

---

The colour coding and the different hose
lengths ensures the hoses cannot be
interchanged at the vacuum units.
Black braided-sheath hoses are used for the
supply lines from the vacuum pump to the
solenoid valves (EUV and EPDW) and to the
vacuum accumulator. Colour-coded braided-
sheath hoses are used for the controlled
supply lines:
Nevertheless, should the vacuum hoses be
confused, the result will be an acute loss of
power.
3 A distributor with four connections is
connected in the vacuum line at the vacuum
pump (1). Three of the connections feature a
∅0.8 mm throttle (2). Only the connection to
the electric changeover valves for the engine
mounts and swirl flaps (11+12) has a
∅0.5 mm throttle. To avoid confusion, this
connection is shorter than the other three. 1

Introduction
Digital Diesel Electronics
DDE 606
The DDE 606 is used in connection with the
M57TU1-TOP engine.
Changes
Compared to the DDE 509 system (M57 TU1
EURO 4), the following actuators have been
added for the two-stage supercharging:
• Electropneumatic pressure converter
(EPDW) for turbine control valve
• Electropneumatic pressure converter
(EPDW) for wastegate
• Electric changeover valve (EUV) for
compressor bypass valve
Performance data:
• Clock frequency: 56 MHz (DDE 509: 40
MHz)
• EEPROM: 8 kB (DDE 509: 2 kB)
• RAM: 32 kB (DDE 509: 32 kB)
There is no air flap control (LKS) on the E60
with the M57TU1-TOP engine.

used for the M57TU engine:

System components
Digital diesel electronics
Sensors/actuators
Sensors
• Accelerator pedal module
• Hot-film air mass meter (HFM)
• Boost pressure sensor
• Coolant temperature sensor
• Fuel temperature sensor
• Rail pressure sensor
• Charge air temperature sensor
• Camshaft position sensor (NWG)
• Thermal oil level sensor (TÖNS)
• Crankshaft position sensor (KWG)
• Exhaust pressure sensor
• Exhaust gas temperature sensor upstream
of oxidation catalytic converter
• Exhaust gas temperature sensor upstream
of diesel particle filter (DPF)
• Oxygen sensor (Bosch LSU 4.9 with
constant characteristic)
Actuators
• Fuel injectors 1-6
• Volume control valve
• Pressure control valve
• Electric changeover valve (EUV) for exhaust
gas recirculation
• Electric changeover valve (EUV) for swirl
flaps
• Electric changeover valve (EUV) for engine
mounts
• E-box fan
• Electric motor throttle valve actuator
• Electropneumatic pressure converter
(EPDW) for turbine control valve
• Electropneumatic pressure converter
(EPDW) for wastegate
• Electric changeover valve (EUV) for
compressor bypass valve
Switches
• Brake light switch/brake light test switch
• Oil pressure switch
• Clutch switch
Relays
• DDE main relay
• Starter relay
Interfaces
• Bit-serial data interface BSD (alternator,
preheating control unit)
• PT-CAN
Electropneumatic pressure converter (EPDW)
Electropneumatic pressure converters
(EPDW) apply vacuum to the diaphragm units
of the turbine control valve and wastegate.
The DDE uses a PWM signal (300 Hz) to
actuate the EPDW. The nominal voltage is
12 V.
Electric changeover valve (EUV)
An electric changeover valve (EUV) applies
vacuum to the diaphragm unit of the
compressor bypass valve. The DDE controls
the EUV. The nominal voltage is 12 V.
